sub: Flirty dares, daring confessions, mischief with a wink.

1.name: Warm-Up
q: What's the most useless skill you're weirdly proud of?
What's your 2am order — and are you proud of it?
What's the most embarrassing thing currently sitting in your phone's predictive text?
What's a rumour about yourself you kind of wish were true?
Guess one thing about them right now, out loud — then find out how close you got.
What's your most confidently wrong opinion?
Pick the song that's basically your personality tonight, then play the first ten seconds.
What's the last thing you searched that you'd rather not explain?
What's your go-to excuse for leaving somewhere early?
What's the boldest thing on your bucket list you'll actually say out loud right now?
What's a compliment you give yourself that genuinely nobody asked for?
What's the worst gift you've ever convincingly faked liking?
What's your go-to photo trick — the angle, the filter, the pose — and does it actually work?
What app on your phone would you be most embarrassed for them to scroll through?
What's a completely made-up fact you told someone with total confidence?
What's the childhood nickname you're weirdly glad never followed you into adulthood?
What's your signature 'I'm fine, everything's fine' face? Do it right now.
What's the most unearned confidence you carry about something you're actually just mediocre at?
What's the one screenshot on your phone you'll never delete but would never explain?
What's the last white lie you told that was almost insultingly easy?
What's your type, in exactly one word — no elaborating allowed?
What's a compliment you're quietly fishing for tonight, whether or not you'll admit it?
What's the most grown-up purchase you've ever made that you're secretly proud of?
What's the emoji you overuse, and what does it actually say about you?
What's a talent you'd list on a dating profile that is a complete and total lie?
What's the pettiest personal record you hold that nobody has ever actually asked about?

2.name: Cheeky
q: Rate your own flirting, smooth to disaster, and defend the score.
What's the smoothest thing you've ever said — and did it actually land?
What's your tell — the thing you do without noticing when you actually like someone? Guess if they've already clocked it.
Confess the most obvious signal you completely missed.
What's the most chaotic thing you've ever done to get someone's attention — and did it even work?
What's the pettiest thing you've ever been genuinely smug about?
What's the most ridiculous reason you've ever been annoyed at someone you actually liked?
What's a text you've drafted, reread, and never sent?
What's the most outrageous thing you've ever done for a discount, an upgrade, or a free anything?
What's the hot take you will absolutely die on that's actually cost you a friend or two?
What's the cheapest thing you've ever done that you'd 100% do again, no shame?
What's the boldest opening line you've ever actually used on someone — and did it land?
What's the most convincing fake confidence you've ever pulled off in the moment?
What's a rule you break specifically because you know you'll get away with it?
What's the recovery move you pull when you say something painfully awkward out loud?
What's the most petty scorekeeping you still secretly do in a friendship or relationship?
What's the most persuasive you've ever been in service of a genuinely terrible idea?
What favour have you asked for that you had absolutely no right to ask for — and got anyway?
What's a compliment you'd never fish for but would absolutely take?
What's a lie you've told so many times you've started to believe it a little yourself?
What's the most unbothered you've ever pretended to be while internally losing it?
What's the boldest DM or text you've ever sent, fully unprompted?
What's a skill you oversold in an interview, on a date, or both?
What's the most chaos you've ever caused purely to make someone laugh at exactly the right moment?
What's the last compliment that actually got to you, even though you played it cool?
What's the most main-character outfit you've ever fully committed to?
What's a secret talent you specifically deploy to impress people?

3.name: Bold
q: Confess the cockiest thing you genuinely believe about yourself — the one you'd never just announce.
What's the boldest lie you've ever gotten away with as an adult — does anyone still not know the truth?
Growing up, what's the boldest lie you told that your parents still, to this day, have no idea about?
What's the most main-character thing you've ever done in public, entirely on purpose?
What's a rumour you'd genuinely love someone to start about you? Guess what they think it'd be, before you answer.
Confess your most unhinged theory about me — it has to be kind enough to say to my face.
What's the cheekiest thing you ever got away with at school or work, zero regrets?
What's something you'd tell a total stranger before you'd tell your best friend — and why do you think that is?
Deliver the single most arrogant sentence you can say about yourself, dead serious, no cracking a smile until you're done.
What's the boldest thing you've ever talked your way into that you absolutely did not deserve?
If a tabloid ran one true headline about you tonight, what would it say?
What's the most shameless thing you've done to get someone's number — and did it actually work?
What's the boldest yes you've ever said with zero explanation attached?
What's the audacious rumour about yourself you'd never bother correcting if it started spreading right now?
What's the cockiest text you've ever sent, and did your confidence survive the reply?
Give your best 'so, funny story…' opener for something you've genuinely never told anyone in this room — you don't have to finish it. Unless you want to.
What's the most 'I can't believe I actually said that' sentence that's ever come out of your mouth?
What's a reputation you've got that's completely undeserved — and are you doing anything to fix it?
What's the boldest thing you've ever done purely to win, even though nothing was actually on the line?
What's the most audacious thing you've ever said to talk your way out of trouble?
Confess the moment you were the most shameless version of yourself — would you do it again?
What's the confidence move you pull out only when it actually, really matters?
What's a line you've been dying to use on someone all night? Say it now, full commitment.
What's the boldest possible compliment about yourself that you actually, secretly, agree with?
What's the most trouble you've ever caused on purpose, just because you knew you could?
What's the wildest thing you've ever talked someone else into doing on a dare?
Last one before Bold's done with you — what's the legend you'd want told about tonight?

wild: Give a ten-second sales pitch for the most useless object within reach — full enthusiasm, no breaking character.
Do your best impression of the last person you texted. They guess who it is.
Swap phones for fifteen seconds. Change one thing on their home screen before you swap back.
Give them a nickname on the spot — it's in effect for the rest of the night, no appeals.
Confess one small lie you've told tonight, right now — then say what actually happened.
Guess their most-used emoji, then check their phone to see if you're right.
Say the single most confident, over-the-top thing you can about yourself. They have to cheer like you just won something.
Pick an accent. You're stuck in it for the next three cards — they call it the second you slip.
Text a friend — not an ex, not your boss — the exact emoji combo they pick for you, zero context. Read the reply out loud the moment it lands.
Give a fully confident, quietly ridiculous fifteen-second alibi for something you obviously did. Zero evidence, total conviction.
Give them full control of your music for the next thirty seconds — whatever they play, you vibe to it, no complaints.
Design their entrance for the next time they walk into a room. They have to demo it immediately, full commitment.
Give a fifteen-second toast for them, right now — half compliment, half loving roast.
Give a dead-serious, five-star product review of the person across from you — full corporate-review voice. They read it back out loud when you're done.
Order an imaginary coffee like it's the final scene of a telenovela — full drama, no words. They guess what you ordered.
Predict, in one sentence, exactly where they'll be in five years. Say it like you're already certain.
final: Before you say anything — look at them. Actually look, for a second longer than feels normal.
Now say the nicest true thing you've got left. No joke to hide behind yet.
There — you've earned the joke back. Ruin the moment properly, then go be trouble together.
